description: Interactive predict-then-weigh lab for NCERT Grade 6 Science, Chapter 6 'Materials Around Us', Activity 6.8 (topic M3.S5.T8, concept C25). The student first RANKS three identical half-filled cups (water / sand / pebbles) from lightest to heaviest, then places each on a digital balance and watches the gram reading count up, then compares any two cups on a two-pan beam balance. Mass is defined only after the prediction is committed. Targets the size-vs-mass misconception. Fully responsive — fluid from ~320px phones to laptops, with two-column landscape layouts on wide screens; sizes to its container so it fits any modal, iframe, or column without horizontal scroll.
curriculum_link: CBSE/NCERT Grade 6 Science — Chapter 6.3.5 'How heavy or light?' / Activity 6.8

objectives.text: Infer which material is heavier or lighter from the readings.
Define mass as the property that tells how heavy or light an object is.

responsive.approach: Container-query based via ResizeObserver — the tool measures its own container (not the window), so it adapts correctly inside any modal, iframe, or column.
responsive.guarantees: Scoped box-sizing reset and svg/img max-width:100% prevent any horizontal overflow; the card is capped at 980px and centered.

breakpoints.wide: >= 700px — landscape: two-column layouts for Predict, Weigh and Compare

interaction_flow: PREDICT: drag each cup into a Lightest / In-between / Heaviest box (tap-to-place fallback). Reveal is locked until all three are ranked.
WEIGH: pick a cup, press Weigh; the cup drops onto a digital balance and the gram reading counts up; each reading fills a record table.
RESULT: prediction is checked slot-by-slot; a two-pan balance compares any two chosen cups (heavier side dips); mass is defined; stars/confetti on a perfect ranking.

cups.description: Override the three cups. Each item: { id, label, material, emoji, mass (grams shown on the balance, cup+contents), accent (hex), scene ('water'|'sand'|'pebbles'), reason (one-line chapter explanation) }. The true order is computed from mass, so any masses work as long as they are distinct.

unit.description: Mass unit label. Readings ≥ 1000 auto-display in kg on the balance.

showCompare.description: Show the two-pan compare balance on the result screen.

finalMessage.default: Mass tells how heavy or light an object is…
finalMessage.description: The 'What is mass?' takeaway shown on the result screen.

default_activity_6_8.description: Use the built-in water / sand / pebbles cups — omit additionalProps entirely.

cups.label: Water
cups.reason: Water holds less matter in the cup than sand or pebbles.

cups.label: Sand
cups.reason: Sand packs more matter than water.

cups.label: Pebbles
cups.reason: Pebbles pack the most matter, so the most mass.

definition_only.description: Override just the closing definition; hide the compare balance.

additionalProps.finalMessage: Mass measures how much matter is in an object — not how big or full it looks.
